raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 1.0,
    "R1.5": 0.0,
    "R1.6": 0.0,
    "R1.7": 1.0,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The three-cluster taxonomy (general PPLs, calibration/selective-prediction, Bayesian NL semantics) must be logically exhaustive and the claimed orthogonality of QuPPL to each cluster must hold under scrutiny; gaps or invalid contrasts undermine the positioning argument.",
    "R1.3": "Strong factual claims — e.g., 'no prior work occupies this point,' 'the first PPL in which this decomposition is a language-level construct' — must be verified against the actual literature; unsubstantiated claims make the novelty case collapse.",
    "R1.4": "Positioning against prior work is precisely novelty assessment: the agent must evaluate whether the claimed delta (first-class interpretation-uncertainty, two-axis abstain grounded in the decomposition) is genuinely unoccupied in the design space. This is the load-bearing responsibility; failure here invalidates the delegation.",
    "R1.7": "Accurate and comprehensive coverage of the three prior-work clusters is essential — missing a closely related PPL or a selective-prediction paper that already separates uncertainty types would fatally weaken the novelty claim. Citation accuracy and coverage are load-bearing for any positioning task."
  },
  "clarification_needed": false,
  "clarification_question": ""
}

rationale.R1.2: The three-cluster taxonomy (general PPLs, calibration/selective-prediction, Bayesian NL semantics) must be logically exhaustive and the claimed orthogonality of QuPPL to each cluster must hold under scrutiny; gaps or invalid contrasts undermine the positioning argument.
rationale.R1.3: Strong factual claims — e.g., 'no prior work occupies this point,' 'the first PPL in which this decomposition is a language-level construct' — must be verified against the actual literature; unsubstantiated claims make the novelty case collapse.
rationale.R1.4: Positioning against prior work is precisely novelty assessment: the agent must evaluate whether the claimed delta (first-class interpretation-uncertainty, two-axis abstain grounded in the decomposition) is genuinely unoccupied in the design space. This is the load-bearing responsibility; failure here invalidates the delegation.
rationale.R1.7: Accurate and comprehensive coverage of the three prior-work clusters is essential — missing a closely related PPL or a selective-prediction paper that already separates uncertainty types would fatally weaken the novelty claim. Citation accuracy and coverage are load-bearing for any positioning task.
